subject: Upgrade to Java 11
commit_message:

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

A lot of test failures when running mvn install.
May need to add @PowerMockIgnore to allow illegal access.
Other class path issues I am not sure on.

Patch Set 1:

Also, ideally we should move all of the example classes out of the src/main/java space as these are really test classes. Anything with a main method is not needed in the final jar.
Patch Set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2:

(2 comments)

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> A lot of test failures when running mvn install.
> May need to add @PowerMockIgnore to allow illegal access.
> Other class path issues I am not sure on.

This has been the behavior earlier as well and has nothing to do with Java 11 upgrade related changes. You can see the same in the successful Jenkins builds from the past.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Also, ideally we should move all of the example classes out of the src/main/java space as these are really test classes. Anything with a main method is not needed in the final jar.

Fiachra, I agree, however I will confine myself to the Java 11 upgrade and not delve into these as I do not know the history why they were put in the "main" folder in the first place. I guess Mandar Sawant can throw some light on the implementation history.

Nope. The exceptions thrown from the tests are one thing but with Java11 some with fail to compile.
Run mvn clean install locally.

Patch Set 3:

Hi Fiachra,
  
 The issues related to clasnotfoundexception and others are fixed and a local build works fine. In gerrit, the ONAP Jobbuilder is using 2 different jenkins jobs - java 8 and java 11. Java 8 fails and Java 11 succeeds. However the job builder gives a -1. Do I abandon this change and create a new one? Wondering why the java 8 jenkins job is still there.

Regards,
Ravi

Seems the old jobs don't get removed. Contacting integration team now regarding this.
